items.prompt: Design a multimodal study predicting platinum response in ovarian cancer from pathology, clinical data and tumor sequencing.
items.reference_answer: Define intended use, cohort/time zero/outcome, patient-level multimodal linkage, leakage-safe preprocessing, modality missingness, locked validation, calibration/subgroups, biological analyses, governance and prospective utility testing.

items.prompt: Turn that platinum-response project into a staged work plan with stop/go criteria.
items.reference_answer: Stage feasibility/data audit, locked retrospective development, independent validation, silent prospective evaluation and impact study; define sample/QC, calibration, subgroup and safety thresholds plus governance at each gate.

items.prompt: The pathology images and genomics were collected from overlapping but nonidentical patients. How do you build cohorts?
items.reference_answer: Create a patient/specimen linkage table, define modality-specific and complete-case cohorts, prevent duplicated patients across splits, characterize missingness and selection, and use architectures/analyses appropriate to available modalities.

items.prompt: Propose a leakage audit for the multimodal ovarian model.

items.prompt: How would you determine whether image features add value beyond clinical and genomic variables?

items.prompt: The model predicts response, but treatment choice influenced who received platinum. Frame causal limitations.
items.reference_answer: Observed outcome reflects treatment selection and eligibility, so prediction is not automatically a treatment-effect model. Define the estimand, capture confounders, consider comparative causal designs, and avoid prescriptive claims.

items.prompt: The LLM infers 'no residual disease' when the note is silent. Set an extraction policy.
items.reference_answer: Silence maps to not documented/unknown, never negative. Require explicit evidence spans, separate absent from unmentioned, calibrate abstention and route ambiguous cases to human review.

items.prompt: A collaborator wants one model across ovarian, endometrial and cervical cancer. Advise.
items.reference_answer: Start from distinct intended uses, biology, treatments, outcomes and data distributions. Test heterogeneity and tumor-specific performance/calibration; use shared modeling only if justified and retain separate claims/validation.

items.prompt: How would you compare five local LLMs fairly when their context windows and output styles differ?

items.prompt: Recommend an operating boundary for a model strong in extraction but weak in evidence grounding.
items.reference_answer: Allow structured extraction with source spans and human verification; prohibit independent interpretation, citation generation and treatment advice; enforce abstention/escalation, audit sampling and monitored reevaluation.
